title: Citharexylum molle
intro: Citharexylum molle, also known as Yerba de San Nicolas, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Verbenaceae family. Its leaves and stems have been used in traditional medicine for their potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Citharexylum molle include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Citharexylum molle is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in tinctures and capsules to leverage its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Citharexylum molle is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Verbenaceae. This plant is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. It belongs to the genus Citharexylum, which consists of several species of flowering plants. The family Verbenaceae is a large group of flowering plants that includes over 200 species. These plants are commonly known as the verbena family or the caprifolioid family.
